title: The Voice
text:
The clearing again. You expected it this time, which means the dream knew you expected it and made you wrong — the clearing is different, smaller, the light coming from below the moss rather than above it.

She is closer than before. The distance between you is social, the distance between two people who have met enough times to have dispensed with formality.

"You're further along than I expected," she says. Her voice has the quality of something that has been deciding whether to use it for a long time. Not unkind. Appraising.

You try to ask her something. The dream takes your words the way dreams do.

"You don't need to ask," she says. "You already know. You've known since the first encounter." A pause. The moss light shifts. "The question is when you'll stop pretending otherwise. That's always the interesting part."

She turns away before you wake. You get the impression this was deliberate.
